channel.title: Heliski Travel — Field Notes
channel.description: Guides, destination comparisons and planning notes for Iceland heliskiing from Heliski Travel.

item.title: What Is Sea-to-Summit Skiing?
item.description: Sea-to-summit skiing is a single continuous descent from a mountain summit down to the ocean's edge. Here's what it means and where it happens.

item.title: Heliskiing in Greenland: The Complete Guide
item.description: A definitive guide to heliskiing in Greenland, from vast Arctic glaciers and couloirs dropping toward icebergs to the expeditionary style, short April to May season and how to plan it.

item.title: Canada vs Alaska Heliskiing: Which Should You Choose?
item.description: Canada vs Alaska heliskiing compared honestly: snow, terrain, difficulty, weather odds, cost and season — a clear decision framework to pick the right trip.

item.title: Why Iceland Is a Bucket-List Heliski Destination
item.description: Iceland is the rare heliski trip that combines sea-to-summit descents, Arctic light and a real coastal town. Here's why it belongs on your bucket list.

item.title: Heliskiing in the Alps: Where It's Allowed and What to Expect
item.description: A definitive guide to heliskiing in the Alps: where it's allowed across Italy, Switzerland and Austria, why France is restricted, and what a day actually involves.

item.title: How to Book a Heliski Trip: A Step-by-Step Guide
item.description: A clear, step-by-step guide to booking a heliskiing trip — from choosing dates and format to deposits, insurance and what to confirm before you pay.

item.title: Private vs Shared Helicopter Heliskiing: Which Is Right for You?
item.description: Private vs shared helicopter heliskiing compared: cost, group size, flexibility and who each suits, plus a clear framework to choose the right format for you.

item.title: Heliskiing Solo: Can You Go on Your Own?
item.description: Thinking of booking a heli trip alone? Yes — solo heliskiers are common and welcome. How shared packages work, ability grouping, the social upside and practical tips.

item.title: Viking Heliskiing: Packages, Season & How to Book (2027)
item.description: A complete guide to Viking Heliskiing on Iceland's Troll Peninsula — packages, prices, season, terrain and how to book through the authorised agent at no extra cost.

item.title: When Is the Best Time to Go Heliskiing?
item.description: A season-by-season, region-by-region guide to the best time to go heliskiing, from midwinter powder to Iceland's spring and summer options.

item.title: Iceland vs Canada for Heliskiing: Which to Choose?
item.description: Iceland vs Canada heliskiing compared honestly: Canada owns powder, scale and lodge culture; Iceland owns sea-to-summit, spring season and a shorter European trip.

item.title: Getting to North Iceland for Heliskiing
item.description: How to travel to North Iceland for heliskiing on the Troll Peninsula: arriving at Keflavík, reaching Siglufjörður via Akureyri or by road, transfers, timings and journey planning.

item.title: Heliskiing in Iceland vs Alaska: Which Is Right for You?
item.description: An honest comparison of heliskiing in Iceland and Alaska — terrain, difficulty, cost, weather and access — to help you choose the right trip.

item.title: Why Book Heliskiing Through an Agent?
item.description: Why book heliskiing through an authorised agent when it costs exactly the same as direct? An honest look at what you gain, and how Heliski Travel works.

item.title: Avalanche Safety for Heliskiing Explained
item.description: How avalanche safety works in heliskiing: the transceiver, shovel and probe explained, airbag packs, companion rescue basics, and why a proper avalanche course matters.

item.title: Heliskiing in Alaska: The Complete Guide
item.description: Heliskiing in Alaska explained: the Chugach, Valdez and Haines, maritime snowpack, spines, season, cost and who it suits — plus an honest alternative.

item.title: Heliboarding: Can You Go Heliskiing on a Snowboard?
item.description: Yes, snowboarders are welcome on heliski trips. How heliboarding works, terrain, traverses, splitboards, gear and why Iceland suits riders.

item.title: Heliskiing in British Columbia: The Complete Guide
item.description: The definitive guide to heliskiing in British Columbia, Canada, covering the interior ranges, snow, season, cost and how BC compares to Iceland.

item.title: What to Pack for Heliskiing: The Complete Checklist
item.description: The definitive heliskiing packing list: layering, outerwear, gloves, goggles, skis, safety gear and sun protection for a spring trip in Iceland.

item.title: Heliskiing in New Zealand: The Complete Guide
item.description: A definitive guide to heliskiing in New Zealand — Wanaka, the Harris Mountains and the Southern Alps — the standout option for Northern-hemisphere skiers in summer.

item.title: How to Choose a Heliski Operator
item.description: How to choose a heliski operator without getting it wrong: a buyer's checklist covering guides, safety, pilots, pricing and red flags.

item.title: The Heliski Lodge Experience in Iceland | Heliski Travel
item.description: Life off the mountain on a heliski week in Iceland: the 4-star Sigló Hótel on Siglufjörður's harbour, geothermal hot tubs, shared meals, evenings and the complete premium experience.

item.title: Heliskiing in Kashmir & Gulmarg, India | Heliski Travel
item.description: A definitive, honest guide to heliskiing in Kashmir and Gulmarg, India: the Himalayan terrain and altitude, the raw adventurous character, the season and the trade-offs.

item.title: Is Heliskiing Worth It? An Honest Verdict
item.description: Is heliskiing worth the money? An honest verdict on the cost, the risk and who a heli trip is genuinely worth it for.

item.title: How to Plan Your First Heliski Trip
item.description: How to plan your first heliski trip: a clear ten-step roadmap covering ability, destination, timing, operator, package, budget, booking, travel and gear.

item.title: How to Ski Powder: A Practical Guide
item.description: Learn how to ski powder well: a practical technique guide covering stance, flotation, rhythm and the mental shift to master deep snow and off-piste.

item.title: Iceland Heliskiing Operators Compared (2027)
item.description: An honest comparison of the main Iceland heliskiing operators on the Troll Peninsula — how they differ on terrain, format, price and style, and how to choose.

item.title: How Much Does Heliskiing Cost? A 2026 Price Guide
item.description: How much does heliskiing cost? A clear 2026 price guide to heliski cost per day, week packages and what drives the price, from around €3,490 upwards.

item.title: Can an Intermediate Skier Go Heliskiing?
item.description: Yes — a confident intermediate can absolutely heliski. Here is the honest ability check, why guides make it work, and why Iceland suits intermediates.

item.title: Heliskiing in Japan: The Complete Guide
item.description: An honest guide to heliskiing in Japan: the legendary Japow, Hokkaido and the Japanese Alps, why heli access is limited, season timing and who it suits.

item.title: Heliskiing for Beginners: Your First Trip Explained
item.description: New to heliskiing? What ability you truly need, how a first day works, and how guides tailor the terrain so nervous first-timers ski well.

item.title: What Is Guaranteed Vertical Feet in Heliskiing?
item.description: What is guaranteed vertical feet in heliskiing? How the vertical-feet pricing model works, what it measures, a worked Iceland example, and why it is fair to you.

item.title: Heliskiing vs Resort Skiing: Is It Worth the Upgrade?
item.description: Heliskiing vs resort skiing compared honestly: snow, terrain, crowds, cost, fitness and safety, and who should make the jump.

item.title: Heliskiing Terminology: A Glossary
item.description: A plain-English glossary of heliskiing terms, from powder and couloirs to transceivers and guaranteed vertical feet, for newcomers decoding the jargon.

item.title: Best Skis for Heliskiing: A Choosing Guide
item.description: The best skis for heliskiing, explained: waist width, rocker, length and shape for powder, plus renting versus bringing your own for a heli trip in Iceland.

item.title: Iceland in Spring: What to Expect for Heliskiing
item.description: Iceland's heliski season is a spring season, running March to mid-June. Here is what to expect from the weather, daylight and snow conditions across the months.

item.title: What to Expect on Your First Heliski Day | Heliski Travel
item.description: An hour-by-hour walkthrough of your first heliski day in Iceland — the evening before, the morning weather call, the safety briefing, boarding the helicopter, your first run and the rhythm of the day.

item.title: Heliskiing for Groups and Families
item.description: How to plan a group or family heliski trip: the private-helicopter advantage, managing mixed abilities, what family heliskiing really means, and booking logistics for organisers.

item.title: Heliskiing Insurance: What Cover Do You Need?
item.description: A practical guide to heliskiing insurance: why standard travel policies often exclude heli skiing, what to look for, and how to check your cover before you travel.

item.title: Heliskiing in Georgia: The Caucasus Guide
item.description: An honest guide to heliskiing in Georgia and the Greater Caucasus, from Gudauri to Svaneti: the affordability, the terrain, the season and the trade-offs.

item.title: Heliski Photography Tips: Capturing Your Trip
item.description: How to capture great photos and video of your heliski trip in Iceland, safely. Gear, settings, filming powder, cold-weather care and the golden Arctic light.

item.title: Heliskiing in the Andes: Chile & Argentina
item.description: A definitive guide to heliskiing in the Andes — Chile's central Andes and Argentina's Patagonia and Las Leñas — the big-terrain Southern-hemisphere option for Northern-summer skiing.

item.title: How Much Vertical Do You Ski in a Day Heliskiing? (and How Many Runs)
item.description: How much vertical do you ski in a day heliskiing? A typical Iceland day delivers roughly 15,000 to 25,000 vertical feet across 7 to 14 runs. Here is what that really means.

item.title: Heliskiing vs Heli-Assisted Ski Touring
item.description: Classic heliskiing or heli-assisted ski touring? An honest comparison of terrain, fitness, cost, pace and feel to help you choose — with both offered in Iceland.

item.title: Heliskiing vs Cat Skiing vs Ski Touring
item.description: Heliskiing, cat skiing or ski touring? A balanced guide to cost, terrain, weather, fitness and feel to help you pick the right way in.

item.title: How Fit Do You Need to Be for Heliskiing?
item.description: How fit you really need to be for heliskiing, why fitness matters in deep snow, and a sensible 8 to 12 week training plan to get in shape.

item.title: Northern Lights and Midnight Sun Heliskiing in Iceland
item.description: Iceland's March to mid-June season offers two celestial spectacles: the Northern Lights early on and the midnight sun later. Here is how to pick your week.

item.title: Heliskiing in the USA: Beyond Alaska
item.description: Heliskiing in the USA beyond Alaska: the Lower 48 explained — Utah's Wasatch, Colorado and Telluride, Nevada's Ruby Mountains, land-use rules, the season, cost and who it suits, plus an honest alternative.

item.title: Heliskiing in Norway: The Complete Guide
item.description: A complete guide to heliskiing in Norway, from the Lyngen Alps and Lofoten to Arctic sea-to-summit descents, season, terrain and how it works.

item.title: Siglufjörður: A Guide to the Heliski Base Town | Heliski Travel
item.description: A travel guide to Siglufjörður, the historic herring town in North Iceland that serves as the base for Viking Heliskiing on the Troll Peninsula.

item.title: Weather and Down-Days in Heliskiing
item.description: How heliskiing handles weather and down-days: why helicopters need visibility and calm wind, what stops flying, how good operators chase clearer skies, and how the guaranteed-vertical model protects you.

item.title: Iceland vs Norway for Heliskiing: Which to Choose?
item.description: Iceland vs Norway heliskiing compared honestly: two Arctic sea-to-summit destinations weighed on terrain, difficulty, season, cost and vibe — with a clear framework.

item.title: Is Heliskiing Safe? An Honest Guide to the Risks
item.description: Is heliskiing safe? An honest, non-sensational guide to avalanche and helicopter risk, how good operators manage it, and what you can do to stay safer.

item.title: The Best Heliskiing Destinations in the World
item.description: A guide to the best heliskiing destinations for 2026, comparing Canada, Alaska, Iceland, Japan, the Alps, Norway and more.
